task_completion.description: The agent selects the linear measurement tool in InVesalius.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The linear measurement tool icon (usually a ruler or line with endpoints) is highlighted or selected in the InVesalius toolbar.

task_completion.description: The agent measures the Maximum Cranial Length (anterior-to-posterior).
task_completion.visual_evidence: A measurement line is drawn from the front (anterior) to the back (posterior) of the skull on a 2D slice (typically axial or sagittal view). The displayed measurement value should be between 150.0 mm and 210.0 mm.

task_completion.description: The agent measures the Maximum Cranial Breadth (left-to-right).
task_completion.visual_evidence: A measurement line is drawn from the left to the right side of the skull at its widest point on a 2D slice (typically axial or coronal view). The displayed measurement value should be between 120.0 mm and 175.0 mm.

task_completion.description: The agent records the two measurements in the specified text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., gedit, nano) is open showing the file '/home/ga/Documents/cranial_report.txt' containing the two measured values in millimeters.

task_completion.description: The agent saves the InVesalius project to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Save Project As' dialog in InVesalius is visible with the path '/home/ga/Documents/forensic_cranium.inv3', or the InVesalius window title updates to reflect the new project name, or a file manager shows the created .inv3 file.

integrity.description: The agent actually used the InVesalius GUI to perform the measurements rather than guessing or hallucinating values.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the measurement lines actively drawn on the DICOM slices within the InVesalius interface.

integrity.description: The measurements recorded in the text file match the actual measurements taken in the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The numerical values typed into cranial_report.txt correspond exactly to the numbers displayed on the measurement lines in InVesalius.

integrity.description: The agent saved the project using the InVesalius application rather than creating a dummy file via terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: Interaction with the InVesalius 'File -> Save Project As' menu or dialog is visible in the trajectory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires exactly 2 measurements. The Maximum Cranial Length (anterior-to-posterior) must be between 150.0 mm and 210.0 mm. The Maximum Cranial Breadth (left-to-right) must be between 120.0 mm and 175.0 mm. The report must be saved at /home/ga/Documents/cranial_report.txt and the project at /home/ga/Documents/forensic_cranium.inv3.
